entities.title: Workflow Refactor v5 specification
Role kinds define authority and one owner session binds each repository lane
app-analyze reasons over exact documentation, graph, result, review, and process correspondence
Every active requirement has seven typed dimensions
The workflow contract is the only route and reduction registry
Effectiveness assessment is read-only and uses a sealed scoring key
Direct stage ownership
Direct stage ownership capability
The DIRECT primary performs each stage and chooses its route.
Each DIRECT stage consumes the preceding canonical handoff.
DIRECT ownership and owner_session_ref=none persist through the terminal handoff.
Stage skills exchange handoff v4 under DIRECT ownership.
Handoffs bind DIRECT-primary, owner_session_ref=none, repository, wave, and run refs.
The primary uses context and graph surfaces; workflow-orchestrator coordinates repository lanes only.
DIRECT stage delegation, a non-none DIRECT owner session, or L1 stage action is an ownership violation.
Persistent repository orchestration and owner-session continuity
Persistent repository orchestration and owner-session capability
One persistent repo-L2 owns every delegated stage and route; L1 never owns either.
The repo-L2 depends on one native repo-lane-dispatch.v1 from L1.
One non-none owner_session_ref and repo_ref-to-L2 binding persist across every stage.
L1 uses native collaboration with repo-lane-dispatch.v1 while L3 packets enter and leave through the same repo-L2.
Lane state binds repository, wave, run, and the stable non-none repo-L2 owner session.
L1 coordinates native lanes while repo-L2 integrates stages, graph MCP, and L3-only subagents.
A missing or replaced repo-L2, L1 stage action, or owner-session drift blocks the lane.
Bounded L3 dispatch through subagents with completed-result provenance
Bounded L3 dispatch and completed-result admission capability
Repo-L2 invokes every L3 only through subagents.
Selection depends on role kind, specialization, and a bounded packet.
Assignment state advances without transferring stage authority.
dispatch-packet.v3 and result-packet.v2 are the L3 boundaries; app-worker mutation also carries app-task-dispatch.v2.

Completed delegation provenance binds result ref, digest, status, profile, model, checklist, assignment, and authority identity; app-analyze stores one explorer completion record atomically in the analysis event.
Subagents connects repo-L2 to L3 roles while immutable provenance admits only completed bound results and app-analyze admits them only in its single analysis event.
Direct dispatch, an incomplete result, packet identity drift, or a separate app-analyze delegation event is rejected.
Build-bound stage-owner records with immutable run, task, result, and review provenance
Build-bound native process and linked correction capability
Only the stage owner records an event that occurred.
Pre-plan scope is empty, ordinary plan-ready establishes it, correction preserves it through terminal app-plan needs-plan, and only a linked run admits remediation tasks.
Records and terminal tasks are immutable, owner sessions are stable, and correction lineage is acyclic with at most one successor per source run.
process_record_event admits only DIRECT-primary or repo-L2 and enforces the event transition matrix.
Event v3 binds run, owner, stage, status, exact task-spec digests, causes, trace, artifacts, payload digest, and Git provenance.
Compilation enforces ledger and event parity, reviews cover exactly result commits, and handoff_validate reconstructs the current boundary.
L3 authorship, duplicate result, session mismatch, incomplete dependency, invalid or extra reviewed commit, scope drift, historical boundary, or dangling cause is rejected.
Whole-snapshot documentation and graph correspondence analysis
Whole-snapshot documentation and graph correspondence capability
App-analyze compares every declared correspondence surface.
Analysis starts from implemented or no-work on one exact build.
Sources and journal remain read-only; drift requires a new analysis.
One explorer reads named documentation and every graph page with opaque cursor continuation.

Inputs bind sources, decisions, requirements, functionalities, dimension mappings, relations, graph edges, functional-map records, ledger records, artifacts, evidence, tasks, results, reviews, remediation tasks, process-index records, and incoming boundaries.
Analysis correlates documentation, graph, ledger, and native events; transient handoffs and packets enter only through represented refs.
A stale basis or incomplete page set cannot yield a complete result.
Typed bounded semantic analysis result
Typed bounded semantic analysis result capability
One result records coverage, findings, and one route.
The result depends on its exact build, profile, model, checklist, and every categorized input-set digest.
The result is immutable and bound to one analysis event.
app-semantic-analysis-result.v1 defines the structured analysis result boundary.
Required fields bind every categorized input count and digest, coverage count, finding, unmapped ref, remediation task ref, completeness value, and route.
The result is represented by one analysis event while any outgoing handoff remains a typed transient protocol input.
A malformed finding or inconsistent route makes the result incomplete.
Logical consistency terminal meaning
Logical consistency terminal meaning capability
Audited is emitted only when every semantic and process condition is complete.
The terminal event analyzes the immediate predecessor build and is the only later journal delta.
Audited is terminal only for the exact bound snapshot.
Event v3 carries the durable terminal status while handoff v4 carries the typed transient stage result.
Zero contradictions, unmapped refs, routable findings, and open remediation tasks are recorded.
Final compilation reconciles the structured analysis event; an outgoing handoff remains transient unless an opted-in source represents it.
Any remaining gap routes to its corrective stage instead of audited.
Seven typed requirement dimensions
Seven typed requirement dimensions capability
Every active requirement maps all seven dimensions or a sourced not-applicable rationale.
Coverage entries depend on unique kind-compatible entities and evidence refs.
Activating a requirement activates its exact dimension set.
Functional map v4 defines dimension and coverage records.
Each record binds requirement, dimension, entity refs, status, rationale when needed, and evidence.
The compiler reconciles the dimension index and coverage list.
Missing, duplicated, reused, or wrong-kind dimension refs fail closed.
Single build-bound registry for edges, statuses, transitions, findings, handoffs, and publication
Build-bound edge, route, event, finding, handoff, and publication capability
Every edge, status, transition, finding, and outgoing handoff resolves through one registry and exact build.
Edge metadata defines traversal, impact, and cycle policy; blocked otherwise dominates the frozen corrective route priority.
Build bundles are immutable and complete before atomic pointer publication; only audited and blocked are terminal.
Workflow v3 defines edge and process rules; handoff_validate derives identity and validates the current payload.
The registry binds edge semantics; findings and handoffs bind exact subjects, conflicts, owner, refs, payload, route, and build.
Compiler, queries, stages, analysis, and handoff validation share the registry; impact is deduplicated and remediates lineage is traversable.
An unknown edge or transition, forbidden cycle, route mismatch, partial publication, stale build, historical boundary, or payload mismatch is rejected.
Read-only effectiveness assessment with sealed scoring key
Read-only sealed-key effectiveness assessment capability
The assessment agent observes exact records and reports without workflow mutation.
Comparative claims depend on frozen cases, metrics, and baseline.
Assessment observations never change workflow state.
The methodology defines metric and report records.
Records bind run, model, outcome, counts, costs, and provenance.
Assessment consumes workflow outputs while remaining outside workflow authority.
Missing baseline or provenance prevents an improvement claim, not a workflow transition.
